productOverview.title: IronPDF for C++
features: Generate PDFs from HTML in C++
Combine, split, and modify PDFs quickly in C++
Extract text and images from PDFs using C++
paragraphs: The new IronPDF library for C++ will enhance the C++ developer's tool set with robust PDF generation and conversion capabilities. Developers will be able to generate new PDFs from HTML content (from text or from URL), as well as to combine, split, extract, and modify content from existing PDFs.
IronPDF for C++ will help developers create C++ applications that can do all of these PDF processing tasks and more, with speed, precision, control, and excellence.

whyLibrary.title: Why make a C++ PDF Library
paragraphs: C++ is one of the most popular, oldest, and important programming languages in use, being the language of choice in low-level systems and network programming and other domains where performance is critical.
The release of IronPDF for C++ will aid developers in building performant applications that can carry out PDF-related processing tasks.

earlyAccess.title: Early Access to C++ PDF Library
paragraphs: Joining the early access program will allow you to collaborate closely with our engineering team. You will be playing a key role in the development process as you share your early experiences using the C++ PDF library before its official launch.
Your continual feedback after we release the library will be immensely helpful as we release new features and improve on existing features.

projectsIndex.text: This page is routed separately to demonstrate controller-based navigation and shared JSON-backed content.

projects.title: Dashboards and admin platforms
projects.summary: Internal tools and admin systems with responsive UI, role-aware behavior, and dependable data flows.
projects.description: Designed for teams that need operational clarity across analytics, permissions, reporting, and day-to-day product workflows. The emphasis is on making dense interfaces feel light, structured, and fast to navigate.
projects.note: The goal is to make complex operational interfaces feel clear instead of heavy.
deliverables: Responsive dashboard shell with reusable layout regions
Role-based navigation with structured access patterns
Reporting blocks and data cards designed for fast scanning
highlights: Dense information grouped into stable, accessible card systems
Admin flows tuned for clarity across desktop and tablet sizes
Shared templates prepared for future modules without UI drift

projects.title: Business websites and landing pages
projects.summary: Modern marketing websites and company pages with strong presentation, clean responsive behavior, and maintainable frontend structure.
projects.description: Built for brands that need persuasive presentation without sacrificing maintainability. The work balances hierarchy, motion restraint, and flexible sections that can evolve as content grows.
projects.note: Good frontend work should look sharp while staying easy to update later.
deliverables: Hero sections designed for clear value communication
Reusable editorial blocks for stories, proof points, and calls to action
Responsive spacing and typography systems built around consistency
highlights: Fast-loading pages with limited layout shift and restrained scripting
Strong headline hierarchy and readable content density
Accessible navigation and focus states across interaction patterns

projects.title: Custom business systems
projects.summary: Custom platforms with authentication, APIs, reporting, and database design across JavaScript and PHP-based stacks.
projects.description: Used when a product needs more than presentation: structured workflows, authentication, business logic, and dependable data movement between interface and backend.
projects.note: I like projects where the UI and the business rules need to stay tightly aligned.
deliverables: Feature-focused application architecture with clean route boundaries
Authentication and input-validation flows tuned for product requirements
Data-backed reporting and feature delivery across admin and customer views
highlights: Pragmatic fullstack scope from database structure through interface states
Architecture shaped by real business rules rather than generic boilerplate
Delivery optimized for maintainability and future feature growth
